name: Testing with cargo test, Doc Tests, Integration Tests
description: Write and run unit tests, doc tests, and integration tests using Cargo's built-in testing framework.

steps.title: Run all tests
steps.description: Execute the full test suite including unit, doc, and integration tests.
steps.tip: Use 'cargo test -- --nocapture' to see println! output from passing tests.

steps.title: Run a specific test by name
steps.description: Filter tests by name pattern to run only matching tests.

steps.title: Create an integration test file
steps.description: Add an integration test in the tests/ directory that tests your crate as an external consumer.

steps.title: Run tests with coverage using cargo-llvm-cov
steps.description: Generate code coverage reports using the LLVM-based coverage tool.
steps.tip: The HTML report is generated in target/llvm-cov/html/. Open index.html in your browser.

steps.title: Run doc tests only
steps.description: Execute only the documentation examples embedded in your code comments.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Rust Test Workflow
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Running All Tests ==="
cargo test 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== Test with Output ==="
cargo test -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tail -20

echo ""
echo "=== Doc Tests ==="
cargo test --doc 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== Test Commands ==="
echo "All tests:      cargo test"
echo "Specific:       cargo test test_name -- --exact"
echo "Doc tests:      cargo test --doc"
echo "Integration:    cargo test --test integration_test"
echo "With output:    cargo test -- --nocapture"
echo "Coverage:       cargo llvm-cov --html"
echo "Ignored tests:  cargo test -- --ignored"

faq.question: Where do I put unit tests vs integration tests?
faq.answer: Unit tests go in the same file as the code they test, inside a #[cfg(test)] mod tests block. Integration tests go in the tests/ directory and test your crate as an external user would.

faq.question: How do doc tests work?
faq.answer: Code blocks in /// doc comments are compiled and executed as tests by 'cargo test --doc'. They serve as both documentation and tests. Mark non-runnable examples with ```no_run or ```ignore.

faq.question: How do I run tests in parallel vs sequentially?
faq.answer: Tests run in parallel by default. Use 'cargo test -- --test-threads=1' for sequential execution, which is needed when tests share state like a database.
